title: Osteoporosis — overclaim and implied
notes: Synthetic; controlled ground truth. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: The drug reduced fracture risk across all skeletal sites.1 The drug increased bone mineral density at the spine and hip.2

goldClaims.originalText: The drug reduced fracture risk across all skeletal sites.1
goldClaims.text: The drug reduced fracture risk across all skeletal sites

goldClaims.originalText: The drug increased bone mineral density at the spine and hip.2
goldClaims.text: The drug increased bone mineral density at the spine and hip

1.name: BONE-1 (Eriksson 2023)
1.text: Vertebral fractures were significantly reduced with the drug versus placebo (p<0.001). Hip fractures were numerically fewer but the reduction did not reach statistical significance.

2.name: BONE-1 densitometry
2.text: Table 5. Bone mineral density change from baseline (%) — spine: drug +6.8, placebo +0.4; hip: drug +3.9, placebo +0.2.

goldVerdicts.claimText: The drug reduced fracture risk across all skeletal sites
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref establishes a vertebral-fracture reduction only; hip reduction was non-significant. Claim generalises to all sites beyond what is established, without explicit denial (rule 1/3).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The drug increased bone mineral density at the spine and hip
goldVerdicts.rationale: Support is a BMD data table for spine and hip with no narrative sentence (rule 5).
